response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer, but the profile does not match a C++ Capital Markets / Fixed Income role. Hands-on C++ is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a university thesis, far short of the 5–10 years of strong, current C++ on Windows required. There is no SQL Server/Oracle experience (only SQL/MySQL/MongoDB) and no fixed income, derivatives, or investment-banking/capital-markets domain exposure; RIDE Capital was retail investment work on a Node/TypeScript stack. The mandatory and domain requirements are essentially unmet.
strengths: Broad, deep engineering background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Fluent communicator with a track record of teaching, talks, and workshops
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~6 months) versus 5–10 years required on Windows
No SQL Server/Oracle and no fixed income / capital-markets / investment-banking domain
Core stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang, not the role's C++/financial-systems focus
